title: Dopaminergic Disruption Induced by Traumatic Coma: Dopaminergic Pathways Abnormalities and Biomarkers of Recovery Using MRI and 18F-LBT-999 PET

skipped_criteria: Affiliated with or beneficiary of a social security system — no schema field for insurance/social security affiliation.
Signed informed consent provided by participant or trusted representative — consent process cannot be encoded in schema fields.
TBI occurring within the last 30 days — temporal criterion (time since injury) not expressible with available fields.
Motor score (M) < 6 at hospital admission — GCS motor subscore is not a separate schema field; only total GCS is available.
Sedative treatments discontinued for more than 48 hours — medication/treatment history field not available in schema.

Clinically stable (no hemodynamic, respiratory, or metabolic instability requiring specific interventions) — composite clinical stability criterion not directly encodable; partially related to sbp_mmhg/hr_bpm but requires clinical judgment beyond simple thresholds.
TBI-COMA Group: no recovery of consciousness at inclusion (GCS < 10 with M < 6) — subgroup stratification by consciousness recovery status and motor subscore not encodable.
TBI-REC Group: recovery of consciousness evidenced by simple command-following (GCS ≥ 10 with M = 6) at inclusion — subgroup stratification by command-following and motor subscore not encodable.
Healthy Controls matched by age (± 2 years) and sex to TBI-COMA patients — matching logic for control group cannot be expressed in per-patient schema rules.
Contraindications to MRI — no schema field for MRI contraindications.
Known allergy to the PET radiotracer or its excipients — no schema field for drug allergies.
History of conditions affecting the dopaminergic system — no schema field for prior medical history.
Individuals under legal protection measures — no schema field for legal status.
Current treatment with dopaminergic agonists or antagonists — no schema field for current medications.
Coma due to causes other than TBI — partially encoded via presumed_tbi and mechanism exclusion of penetrating trauma, but non-TBI coma etiologies (e.g., metabolic, anoxic) cannot be fully enumerated.
Decompressive craniectomy resulting in anatomical alterations incompatible with standardized image analysis (e.g., midline shift > 2 cm) — no schema field for surgical history or imaging findings.
Healthy Controls only: women of childbearing potential without effective contraception — subgroup-specific criterion and contraception status not encodable in schema.
Healthy Controls only: women unwilling to maintain effective contraception during the 30-day study period — patient willingness/compliance criterion not encodable in schema.
Breastfeeding women excluded — no schema field for breastfeeding status.
